content.problemStatement:
You are given an integer NNN.

Print `THALA` if N=7N=7N=7, otherwise print `SADGE`.


Input Format

The only line of input contains an integer NNN.


Output Format
Output a single string, print `THALA` if N=7N=7N=7, otherwise print `SADGE`.


You may print each character of the string in uppercase or lowercase (for example, the strings `THALA`, `thALa`, `thala`, and `tHalA` will all be treated as identical).
content.inputFormat: The first line contains an integer T, the number of test cases. Each of the next T lines contains a single integer N.
content.outputFormat: For each test case output a single line containing "THALA" if N equals 7, otherwise output "SADGE".

sampleTestCases.explanation: There are two test cases. For N=7 the condition holds, so output THALA. For N=1 it does not, so output SADGE.
All three test cases have N equal to 7, therefore each line prints THALA.
The single test case has N=42 which is not 7, so the output is SADGE.

solution.approach: Read the number of test cases T. Loop T times reading N. For each N check if N==7. If true print THALA, else print SADGE. This is O(1) work per test case.
solution.timeComplexity: O(T) – constant work for each of the T test cases.
solution.spaceComplexity: O(1) – only a few variables are used regardless of input size.

code.Python3:
import sys

def solve():
    data = sys.stdin.read().strip().split()
    if not data:
        return
    t = int(data[0])
    out = []
    idx = 1
    for _ in range(t):
        n = int(data[idx]); idx+=1
        out.append('THALA' if n==7 else 'SADGE')
    sys.stdout.write('\n'.join(out))

if __name__ == "__main__":
    solve()
